title: Phalocallis oreophila
intro: Phalocallis oreophila is a type of medicinal plant, also known as Himalayan gentian or gentian, which is a flowering plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion and ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Phalocallis oreophila include gentiopicroside, amarogentin, and gentianine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phalocallis oreophila is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Phalocallis oreophila is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is endemic to the Himalayas and is also known as Himalayan gentian. The plant belongs to the genus Phalocallis, which is a small genus of flowering plants. Further classification of Phalocallis oreophila includes its classification as a dicotyledonous plant, belonging to the subclass Asteridae. The plant is further classified into various ranks, including family, genus, species, and others.
